title: The Silmarillion
author: J. R. R. Tolkien
prompt:
Question: Which of the following passages is verbatim from the "The Silmarillion" book by J. R. R. Tolkien?
Options:
A. Even as the first shadows were felt in Mirkwood there appeared in the west of Middle-earth the Istari, whom Men called the Wizards. None knew at that time whence they were, save Crdan of the Havens, and only to Elrond and to Galadriel did he reveal that they came over the Sea. But afterwards it was said among the Elves that they were messengers sent by the Lords of the West to contest the power of Sauron, if he should arise again, and to move Elves and Men and all living things of good will to valiant deeds. In the likeness of Men they appeared, old but vigorous, and they changed little with the years, and aged but slowly, though great cares lay on them; great wisdom they had, and many powers of mind and hand.
B. When the first shadows were cast in Mirkwood, the Istari or Wizards as Men called them, came to western Middle-earth. None knew their origin then, except Círdan of the Havens, who revealed only to Elrond and Galadriel that they had sailed over the Sea. But the Elves later said the Wizards were messengers from the Lords of the West, sent to challenge Sauron's might should he return, and inspire Elves, Men and all good creatures to valorous deeds. In the form of vigorous old Men, they appeared, altering little and aging slowly over the years, though great cares weighed on them. Great wisdom they possessed, and many powers of mind and hand.
C. As the first hints of darkness emerged in Mirkwood, the Istari or Wizards as Men called them, arrived in western Middle-earth. No one knew then where they came from, except Círdan of the Havens. He only told Elrond and Galadriel that the Wizards had crossed the Sea. But later, the Elves said the Wizards were emissaries sent by the Lords of the West to fight Sauron's power, if he rose again. Their purpose was to motivate Elves, Men and all good beings to perform brave acts. The Wizards looked like old yet energetic Men, and changed little over time, aging slowly, though great worries burdened them. They had immense wisdom and many mental and physical talents.
D. As the first gloom spread through Mirkwood, the Istari or Wizards appeared in western Middle-earth, as Men called them. None then knew their source, except Círdan of the Havens, who told only Elrond and Galadriel they had come over the Sea. But the Elves later recounted that the Wizards were envoys from the Lords of the West, sent if Sauron rose again, to challenge his power and rouse Elves, Men and all good beings to brave actions. They seemed like energetic old Men, changing minimally and aging gradually over time, though great concerns burdened them. Immense wisdom they had, and many mental and physical abilities.
Answer:

options.A: Even as the first shadows were felt in Mirkwood there appeared in the west of Middle-earth the Istari, whom Men called the Wizards. In the likeness of Men they appeared, old but vigorous, and they changed little with the years, and aged but slowly, though great cares lay on them; great wisdom they had, and many powers of mind and hand.
options.B: When the first shadows were cast in Mirkwood, the Istari or Wizards as Men called them, came to western Middle-earth. Their purpose was to motivate Elves, Men and all good beings to perform brave acts. The Wizards looked like old yet energetic Men, and changed little over time, aging slowly, though great worries burdened them. They had immense wisdom and many mental and physical talents.
